how_verified: In isolation per the law (NOT importing the whole server). Constructed a throwaway FastMCP('test') + a real Suite(FsStore(STORE_DIR), NodeRegistry().discover(['nodes'])) mirroring server.py:20-21, captured node = node_mod.register(mcp, suite), and fired a sample of EACH op + the error paths. `company gpu` first (chat-4b + embed-bge co-resident) so propose's brain call was real. Results: (1) unknown op 'frobnicate' -> teaching error listing the 4 valid ops. (2) op='create' on a scratch graph with type='constant' -> {op,graph,type,node_id:'constant-1',address:'run://.../constant-1'}; missing-type -> teaching error. (3) op='delete' that node_id -> {ok:True}; missing node_id -> teaching error. (4) op='propose' (name='shout_test', spec='uppercase + 3 exclamations') -> real brain wrote a node module, returned {surfaced_id:'s95-code_build', name, code}; missing spec -> teaching error. (5) FLOOR PROOF: op='apply' on that UNAPPROVED surfaced_id -> GovernanceError: "action 'code_build' requires confirmation (CONFIRM); surfaced for the operator" (refused through the consolidated tool, exactly as the flat tool); op='apply' missing surfaced_id -> teaching error; op='apply' on nonexistent id -> KeyError 'no surfaced decision' (fail-loud pass-through). Scratch graph (.data/store/graphs/node-tool-verify-scratch.json + its lock) deleted after.
notes: FLOOR CARE CONFIRMED + PROVEN: node(op=apply) introduces NO new escalation path. apply_node's authorization is READ from the substrate inside Suite.apply_node via guard('code_build', confirmed=self.inbox.is_approved(surfaced_id)) (suite.py:8763) — never a caller flag. This wrapper passes ONLY `surfaced_id` through; no field on the tool can reach `confirmed`. Empirically proven: apply on an unapproved id RAISES GovernanceError (not faked-green; the refusal IS the pass). propose_node SURFACES with default='reject' and never applies (suite.py:8733-8736), so it is safe for a cold external agent. The floor stays exactly where it was — inside the Suite, not the face. SIGNATURE: the task sketch node(op,graph,type,config,node_id) only covered create/delete; propose needs name+spec and apply needs surfaced_id, so the real signature is node(op, graph='', type='', config={}, node_id='', name='', spec='', surfaced_id=''). Per-op required-param validation with teaching errors carries the steering since the instance-ops (create/delete) and type-ops (propose/apply) take DISJOINT param sets. No `detail` param (none of these are heavy reads — that is corpus-specific, not cargo-culted here). No re-validation of `type` (create_node already raises a teaching KeyError listing list_types()). Returns high-signal dicts {op, node_id/address/surfaced_id/path} not raw 'ok' strings, matching corpus.py. NOT in this lane (kept distinct per the audit's trade-off, NOT folded): connect, set_config, run_graph, get_state, get_results — graph-edge/config/run/read verbs, separate concerns. FILE-DISJOINT honored: wrote ONLY mcp_face/tools/node.py; did NOT touch server.py (flat-tool removal is the serial pass — see flat_tools_to_remove). A real surfaced item s95-code_build remains in the inbox from the propose verification (unapproved, default=reject) — left honest for the operator to reject, NOT force-resolved (forging a resolution would violate the floor). NOT committed (per instruction).
